mutators.name: startObservationAddSelection
mutators.semantics: Records a user-scoped pending add-observation selection from the assets selectable by the actor's locally observed update-access decision.

mutators.name: confirmObservationAddSelection
mutators.semantics: Consumes the pending selection exactly once and creates an asset-query payload containing only assets visible to the actor at confirmation; inaccessible selected assets are silently omitted even when no redirect payload can be produced.

mutators.name: recordObservation
mutators.semantics: Appends an observation whose asset field is exactly the supplied raw asset ID list, without replacing it with a loaded, viewable-only subset, and whose quantity field may contain zero or more revision-aware quantity references.

mutators.name: cloneObservation
mutators.semantics: Appends a cloned observation and a distinct cloned quantity for every quantity revision referenced by the source, so the clone references new quantity entities rather than the source quantities.

mutators.name: recordQuantityDeletion
mutators.semantics: Records deletion of a quantity and revision updates removing only that quantity from every referencing observation, including otherwise undisclosed observations; each affected observation revision carries a message identifying the deleted quantity UUID.

mutators.name: recordObservationDeletion
mutators.semantics: Records deletion of the observation together with deletion of every quantity currently referenced by that observation's quantity field.

projections.name: getObservationAssetPrepopulation
projections.semantics: Returns the raw asset query IDs for a new observation create form and returns an empty list for an edit form, so query parameters never prepopulate an existing observation.

projections.name: getConfirmedObservationAssetQuery
projections.semantics: Returns the viewable-only asset IDs produced by confirmation, or an empty list once the query payload is no longer available under the as-of log state.

projections.name: getObservation
projections.semantics: Returns the observation state at or before the query time, including its ordered asset references, status, and zero-or-more QuantityRevisionHandle references; returns null when deleted or not disclosed.
projections.derived_from: invented: a single materialized observation view is required to expose the asset and multi-value revision-aware quantity fields maintained by the entity and field hooks.

projections.name: listObservationRevisions
projections.semantics: Returns the observation's revision history known at the query time, including quantity-removal revisions and their deleted-quantity UUID messages.

projections.name: listObservationsForAsset
projections.semantics: Returns observations referencing the asset in the log-query ordering at or before the query time, filtered to records disclosed to this replica.

projections.name: getFirstObservationForAsset
projections.semantics: Returns the first observation for the asset under the same materialized log-query ordering as listObservationsForAsset, or null when none exists.

projections.name: getDefaultQuantityType
projections.semantics: Returns the quantity type selected as the default by the materialized quantity configuration at the query time, or null when no default exists.

rationale: Writes are represented as domain events: asset-selection confirmation, observation recording and cloning, and observation/quantity deletion. Reads are as-of materialized views because later creation, revision, deletion, configuration, and disclosure events can supersede prior values. Confirmation and prepopulation are separated to preserve both subtle asset behaviors: confirmation silently filters by visibility and consumes its selection, while a newly recorded observation preserves the raw submitted asset list exactly and edit forms never prepopulate from that query. Quantity references are explicitly plural and revision-aware; cloning and both deletion cascades are therefore expressed as event-producing operations, with revision history exposed to verify the required deletion message.
